title: The Thirsty Crow
moral: Where there is a will, there is a way. Hard work and clever thinking can solve any problem!

scenes.line: Once upon a time, a crow was flying over the land.
It was a very hot summer day.
The crow was very, very thirsty.
He searched everywhere for water.
The rivers were all dried up.
The ponds had no water left.
Then he saw a pot near a house!
He flew down quickly to drink.
But the water was at the very bottom.
His beak could not reach the water.
He tried and tried but it was too deep.
Then he had a brilliant idea!
He saw small stones lying nearby.
He picked up a stone with his beak.
He dropped the stone into the pot.
The water level rose a tiny bit!
He dropped another stone, and another.
One by one, the stones went in.
Slowly, the water came up higher.
At last, the water reached the top!
The happy crow drank the cool water.
He was no longer thirsty!

title: The Tortoise and the Hare
moral: Slow and steady wins the race. Never underestimate others!

scenes.line: In a green meadow lived a hare and a tortoise.
The hare was the fastest animal around.
He loved to boast about his speed.
One day he laughed at the slow tortoise.
You are so slow! You can never beat me!
The tortoise smiled and said, Let us race!
All the animals gathered to watch.
Ready, set, GO! The race began!
The hare zoomed ahead in seconds.
The tortoise walked slowly but steadily.
The hare was so far ahead, he got bored.
I have plenty of time, let me rest here.
The hare fell fast asleep under a tree.
Meanwhile, the tortoise kept walking.
Step by step, he moved forward.
He passed the sleeping hare quietly.
The hare woke up and stretched.
Where is the tortoise? he wondered.
He looked ahead and saw the finish line!
The tortoise was almost there!
The hare ran as fast as he could!
But it was too late!
The tortoise crossed the finish line first!
All the animals cheered for the tortoise!
The hare learned to never be overconfident.

title: The Lion and the Mouse
moral: Even the smallest friends can be the greatest helpers. Be kind to everyone!

scenes.line: Deep in the jungle lived a mighty lion.
He was the king of all the animals.
One afternoon, the lion was sleeping.
A tiny mouse ran across his paw.
The lion woke up with a ROAR!
He caught the little mouse in his paw.
Please let me go, the mouse begged.
Someday I will help you too!
The lion laughed. You? Help me?
But he was kind and let the mouse go.
Many days later, hunters came to the jungle.
They set a big net to catch the lion.
The lion got trapped in the strong net!
He roared and roared but could not escape.
The little mouse heard his cries.
He ran to the lion as fast as he could.
Do not worry, I will save you!
The mouse began to chew the ropes.
He chewed and chewed with his tiny teeth.
One by one, the ropes broke apart.
The lion was free at last!
Thank you, little friend! said the lion.
The lion and mouse became best friends.

title: The Boy Who Cried Wolf
moral: Nobody believes a liar, even when they tell the truth. Always be honest!

scenes.line: A shepherd boy watched over his sheep.
Every day he sat on the hill alone.
He got very bored with nothing to do.
One day he had a naughty idea.
Wolf! Wolf! he shouted loudly.
The villagers ran up the hill to help.
But there was no wolf at all!
The boy laughed at the worried villagers.
The villagers went back, feeling foolish.
A few days later, the boy did it again.
Wolf! Wolf! A big bad wolf!
The villagers rushed up the hill once more.
Again, there was no wolf anywhere.
The boy was rolling on the ground laughing.
The villagers were very angry this time.
Then one evening, a real wolf appeared!
The wolf started chasing the sheep.
Wolf! Wolf! Please help me!
But nobody came this time.
The villagers thought he was lying again.
The wolf scared away all his sheep.
The boy sat alone and cried.
He learned that liars are never believed.

title: The Ugly Duckling
moral: Never judge anyone by how they look. Everyone is beautiful in their own way!

scenes.line: On a sunny farm, a mother duck sat on her eggs.
One by one, cute little ducklings hatched.
But the last egg was much bigger.
Out came a duckling who looked different.
He was bigger and grey, not yellow.
The other ducklings made fun of him.
You are so ugly! they teased.
Even the farm animals were unkind.
The sad duckling ran away from home.
He wandered alone through the cold winter.
He was lonely and very sad.
Nobody wanted to be his friend.
Days turned into weeks, weeks into months.
Spring arrived with warm sunshine.
The duckling saw beautiful swans on a lake.
He wished he could be as beautiful as them.
He looked down at his reflection in the water.
He could not believe his eyes!
He had grown into a beautiful white swan!
The other swans welcomed him happily.
Children on the shore said, Look at that beautiful swan!
The once ugly duckling was the most beautiful of all!
He finally found where he truly belonged.

title: The Three Little Pigs
moral: Hard work pays off. Take time to do things right and build a strong foundation!

scenes.line: Once there were three little pigs.
They left home to build their own houses.
The first pig was lazy.
He built his house with straw. Quick and easy!
The second pig built a house of sticks.
It was a bit better, but still not strong.
The third pig worked very hard.
He built his house with strong bricks.
It took a long time, but it was solid.
Then one day, a big bad wolf came!
He went to the straw house first.
Little pig, little pig, let me in!
Not by the hair on my chinny chin chin!
The wolf huffed and puffed and blew it down!
The first pig ran to the stick house.
The wolf huffed and puffed again.
The stick house fell apart too!
Both pigs ran to the brick house.
The wolf huffed and puffed with all his might.
But the brick house did not move at all!
He puffed and puffed until he was exhausted.
The wolf gave up and ran away.
The three pigs danced and celebrated!
They all lived happily in the strong brick house.

title: Goldilocks and the Three Bears
moral: Always respect other people's things. Do not take what is not yours!

scenes.line: In a cozy cottage in the woods lived three bears.
There was Papa Bear, Mama Bear, and Baby Bear.
One morning, they went for a walk.
Their porridge was too hot to eat.
A girl named Goldilocks wandered by.
She saw the empty cottage and went inside.
She tasted Papa Bear's porridge. Too hot!
She tasted Mama Bear's porridge. Too cold!
She tasted Baby Bear's porridge. Just right!
She ate it all up!
Then she tried Papa Bear's chair. Too hard!
She tried Mama Bear's chair. Too soft!
She sat in Baby Bear's chair. Just right!
But the chair broke into pieces!
Upstairs, she tried Papa Bear's bed. Too hard!
Mama Bear's bed was too soft!
Baby Bear's bed was just right!
She fell fast asleep.
The three bears came home.
Someone ate my porridge! said Baby Bear.
Someone broke my chair! he cried.
Someone is sleeping in my bed!
Goldilocks woke up and saw the bears!
She jumped out the window and ran home!
She never went into someone's house again!

title: The Ant and the Grasshopper
moral: Work today for a better tomorrow. Prepare for hard times while you can!

scenes.line: In a beautiful meadow lived an ant and a grasshopper.
It was a warm and sunny summer.
The ant worked hard every single day.
She carried food to store for winter.
Grain by grain, she filled her home.
The grasshopper just sang and danced.
Why do you work so hard? he asked.
Come sing and play with me instead!
I must save food for winter, said the ant.
You should do the same!
The grasshopper laughed. Winter is far away!
Summer passed, and autumn came.
The leaves turned orange and fell.
Then cold winter arrived with snow.
The ant was warm in her cozy home.
She had plenty of food to eat.
The grasshopper was cold and hungry.
He had no food and no shelter.
Please help me, dear ant! he begged.
The kind ant shared her food with him.
The grasshopper thanked her from his heart.
He promised to work hard next summer.

title: The Fox and the Grapes
moral: It is easy to hate what you cannot have. Accept failures gracefully!

scenes.line: A hungry fox was walking through the forest.
He had not eaten anything all day.
His tummy was growling loudly.
Then he saw a tall grapevine.
Beautiful purple grapes hung from it!
They looked so juicy and delicious!
The fox jumped as high as he could.
But the grapes were too high to reach!
He tried again, jumping even higher.
Still too high! He could not reach them.
He took a running start and leaped!
His paws almost touched them, but not quite.
Again and again he jumped.
He was getting tired and frustrated.
Finally, the fox gave up.
He walked away with his nose in the air.
Those grapes are probably sour anyway!
I did not want them in the first place!
A bird sitting on the vine giggled.
She knew the grapes were perfectly sweet!

title: Little Red Riding Hood
moral: Listen to your parents and do not talk to strangers. Stay safe!

scenes.line: A sweet girl wore a red riding hood.
Everyone called her Little Red Riding Hood.
One day, her mother gave her a basket.
Take this to Grandma. She is not feeling well.
And do not talk to strangers!
Red Riding Hood walked through the forest.
She picked pretty flowers along the way.
A big wolf saw her and smiled slyly.
Where are you going, little girl?
To my Grandma's house, she said.
The wolf ran ahead to Grandma's house.
He tricked Grandma and hid in her bed.
Red Riding Hood arrived at the cottage.
Grandma, what big eyes you have!
All the better to see you, my dear!
Grandma, what big ears you have!
All the better to hear you, my dear!
Grandma, what big teeth you have!
All the better to... but just then!
A brave woodcutter heard the noise!
He rushed in and chased the wolf away!
Grandma was safe and sound!
Red Riding Hood hugged her Grandma.
She promised to always listen to her mother.
